title: Number of Operations to Make Network Connected - DSU: G-49.
content:
Problem Statement: You are given a graph with n vertices and m edges. You can remove one edge from anywhere and add that edge between any two vertices in one operation. Find the minimum number of operations that will be required to make the graph connected. If it is not possible to make the graph connected, return -1.

Examples 
Input: N = 4, M = 3, Edge[] =[ [0,  1], [ 0, 2], [1, 2]]


Output: 1
Explanation: We need a minimum of 1 operation to make the two components connected. We can remove the edge (1,2) and add the edge between node 2 and node 3 like the following:


Input: N = 9, M = 8, Edge[] = [[0,1],[0,2],[0,3],[1,2],[2,3],[4,5],[5,6],[7,8]]


Output: 1
Explanation :  We need a minimum of 2 operations to make the two components connected. We can remove the edge (0,2) and add the edge between node 3 and node 4 and we can remove the edge (0,3) and add it between nodes 6 and 8 like the following:

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Approach
Algorithm
Our objective in this question is to make the given graph connected. Now, we can do this by just connecting the different components of a given graph with some edges like the following:

But according to the question, we cannot add random edges from outside rather we can remove any given edge and add that to a new position to fulfill the purpose. In order to connect two different components of a graph we need to connect any node of the first component to any node of the second component. From the method of connecting the components, discussed above, we can conclude that we need a minimum of nuber of components-1 edges to make the graph connected.

Thus we can conclude that if graph has more than n-1 extra edges, then we connect all components.
  • Initialize DSU (Disjoint Set Union) structure to keep track of connected components in the graph.
  • Iterate over all the edges, and for each edge, check if both nodes are already connected using DSU.
  • If the nodes are already connected (i.e., they have the same parent), it means the edge is extra (redundant), so count it as an extra edge.
  • If the nodes are not connected, perform a union operation to connect the components.
  • After processing all edges, count the number of connected components by counting the number of unique parents in DSU.
  • To make the graph connected, we need (components - 1) edges. If the number of extra edges is at least (components - 1), then we can connect the graph using those, otherwise, it’s impossible.
  • Return (components - 1) if possible, else return -1.
Code
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566676869707172737475767778
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Class to manage Disjoint Set Union
class DSU {
public:
    vector<int> parent, rank;

    // Initialize DSU with n nodes
    DSU(int n) {
        parent.resize(n);
        rank.resize(n, 0);

        // Initially, every node is its own parent
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            parent[i] = i;
        }
    }

    // Find with path compression
    int find(int x) {
        if(parent[x] != x)
            parent[x] = find(parent[x]);
        return parent[x];
    }

    // Union by rank
    void unite(int x, int y) {
        int px = find(x);
        int py = find(y);

        if(px == py) return;

        if(rank[px] < rank[py])
            parent[px] = py;
        else if(rank[px] > rank[py])
            parent[py] = px;
        else {
            parent[py] = px;
            rank[px]++;
        }
    }
};

// Solution class to calculate operations
class Solution {
public:
    // Function to find minimum operations to make the graph connected
    int makeConnected(int n, vector<vector<int>>& connections) {
        // If edges are less than n - 1, we can't connect
        if(connections.size() < n - 1) return -1;

        DSU dsu(n);

        // Connect components
        for(auto& edge : connections) {
            dsu.unite(edge[0], edge[1]);
        }

        // Count number of unique components
        unordered_set<int> components;
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            components.insert(dsu.find(i));
        }

        // Return number of operations needed
        return components.size() - 1;
    }
};

// Driver code
int main() {
    int n = 6;
    vector<vector<int>> connections = {{0,1},{0,2},{0,3},{1,4}};
    Solution sol;
    cout << sol.makeConnected(n, connections) << endl;
    return 0;
}

import java.util.*;

class Solution {
    // Find parent with path compression
    private int find(int[] parent, int x) {
        if (parent[x] != x)
            parent[x] = find(parent, parent[x]);
        return parent[x];
    }

    // Union by rank
    private void union(int[] parent, int[] rank, int x, int y) {
        int px = find(parent, x);
        int py = find(parent, y);
        if (px == py) return;

        if (rank[px] < rank[py]) parent[px] = py;
        else if (rank[px] > rank[py]) parent[py] = px;
        else {
            parent[py] = px;
            rank[px]++;
        }
    }
    
    // Function to find minimum operations to make the graph connected
    public int makeConnected(int n, int[][] connections) {
        // If not enough edges, return -1
        if (connections.length < n - 1) return -1;

        int[] parent = new int[n];
        int[] rank = new int[n];

        // Initialize parent
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) parent[i] = i;

        // Union all edges
        for (int[] edge : connections)
            union(parent, rank, edge[0], edge[1]);

        // Count unique parents
        Set<Integer> components = new HashSet<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            components.add(find(parent, i));

        return components.size() - 1;
    }
}

// Main class
public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Solution sol = new Solution();
        int[][] connections = {{0,1},{0,2},{0,3},{1,4}};
        int n = 6;
        System.out.println(sol.makeConnected(n, connections));
    }
}

class DSU:
    def __init__(self, n):
        # Initialize parent and rank
        self.parent = [i for i in range(n)]
        self.rank = [0] * n

    # Find with path compression
    def find(self, x):
        if self.parent[x] != x:
            self.parent[x] = self.find(self.parent[x])
        return self.parent[x]

    # Union by rank
    def union(self, x, y):
        px = self.find(x)
        py = self.find(y)
        if px == py:
            return
        if self.rank[px] < self.rank[py]:
            self.parent[px] = py
        elif self.rank[px] > self.rank[py]:
            self.parent[py] = px
        else:
            self.parent[py] = px
            self.rank[px] += 1

class Solution:
    # Function to find minimum operations to make the graph connected
    def makeConnected(self, n, connections):
        # If not enough edges
        if len(connections) < n - 1:
            return -1

        dsu = DSU(n)

        # Union edges
        for u, v in connections:
            dsu.union(u, v)

        # Count unique components
        components = set()
        for i in range(n):
            components.add(dsu.find(i))

        return len(components) - 1

# Driver code
n = 6
connections = [[0,1],[0,2],[0,3],[1,4]]
sol = Solution()
print(sol.makeConnected(n, connections))
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253545556
class DSU {
    constructor(n) {
        this.parent = Array(n).fill(0).map((_, i) => i);
        this.rank = Array(n).fill(0);
    }

    // Find with path compression
    find(x) {
        if (this.parent[x] !== x)
            this.parent[x] = this.find(this.parent[x]);
        return this.parent[x];
    }

    // Union by rank
    union(x, y) {
        let px = this.find(x);
        let py = this.find(y);
        if (px === py) return;

        if (this.rank[px] < this.rank[py]) this.parent[px] = py;
        else if (this.rank[px] > this.rank[py]) this.parent[py] = px;
        else {
            this.parent[py] = px;
            this.rank[px]++;
        }
    }
}

class Solution {
    // Function to find minimum operations to make the graph connected
    makeConnected(n, connections) {
        // If not enough edges
        if (connections.length < n - 1) return -1;

        const dsu = new DSU(n);

        // Union all edges
        for (let [u, v] of connections) {
            dsu.union(u, v);
        }

        // Count unique components
        const components = new Set();
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            components.add(dsu.find(i));
        }

        return components.size - 1;
    }
}

// Driver code
const n = 6;
const connections = [[0,1],[0,2],[0,3],[1,4]];
const sol = new Solution();
console.log(sol.makeConnected(n, connections));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(N + M × α(N)), we visit every node and edge once to find number of components.
Space Complexity: O(N), for storing parent and rank/size vector for DSU.
